methodology: vulnerable = code at the CVE-fix commit's parent; secure = code at the fix commit; isolated changed file(s); match at file+CWE (CWE-family normalized); paired recall/precision (OWASP-Benchmark style): recall = flags task CWE on vulnerable / 181; precision = TP/(TP+FP) where FP = flagged the FIXED version. Ground truth is real (from CVE fixes), execution-verified upstream, not model-judged.
notes: Thinking-mode tested and rejected: ~2x slower, no recall gain.
Triage tested and rejected on this set: it dropped real TPs (net F1 down).
Absolute recall is low for ALL tools: most real CVEs here are authz / info-exposure / resource / sanitizer-bypass bugs that are not source->sink shaped.
